Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The answer should be provided as text that contains Anomalies identified list the suspicious activities They are R to P Receive to Approve has average time 25 hours with a very low standard deviation indicating a rigid schedule P to N Approve to Notify has an extremely long average delay of 7 days high variability A to C Assign to Close has a relatively quick closure 2 hours average which might indicate premature closure without evaluation approval steps E to N Evaluate to Notify only 5 minutes on average which is suspiciously fast

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Generate hypotheses reasons could be system delays manual data entry automated process skipping required checks bottlenecks inconsistent resource availability etc Hypotheses may include For R to P anomaly the process might have a fixed schedule with approvals occurring at predetermined times or automatic batch processing For P to N anomaly perhaps there is a delay in notification sending due to manual steps workload constraints delays in communication channels or system issues For A to C anomaly some claims are being closed without proper evaluation this might be due to automated triggers that close minor claims quickly or due to errors missing evaluate approve For E to N anomaly the process may have integrated a fast notification system that does not allow time for further checks or it may be auto generated notifications

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can propose some SQL queries using PostgreSQL functions like EXTRACT EPOCH FROM timestamp difference We will propose separate queries for anomalies for R to P average 90000 seconds low standard deviation P to N average 604800 seconds with large variability A to C suspicious quick closure and E to N very fast notification

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We want to find anomalies where times fall outside expected ranges for R to P and P to N and we can filter claims based on time differences from the expected average plus or minus a threshold based on the standard deviation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: A to C is flagged as suspicious because it may indicate skipping intermediate steps so we can look for claims where evaluate or approve are missing entirely after assign and before close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So a query can detect claims that have a closing event shortly after assignment but no evaluation and approval between them and another query can detect E to N cases where the time is below a small threshold indicating a too quick transition

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: If it is too quick maybe less than a threshold say less than 3600 seconds then we can suspect anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I can generate a summary in plain text listing anomalies possible reasons and sample SQL queries I will produce a final answer with clear structure first list anomalies then list hypotheses with bullet points then provide sample queries for each anomaly detection
